questions.question: Which ARIA attribute indicates an accordion section is expanded?
questions.explanation: aria-expanded="true/false" communicates the open/closed state of an accordion panel to assistive technologies.

questions.question: What is the required keyboard pattern for tab panels?
options: Arrow keys to navigate between tabs
questions.explanation: ARIA Authoring Practices recommend Arrow keys (Left/Right or Up/Down) to navigate between tabs, with Tab entering/leaving the tablist.

questions.question: Which role should a modal container have?
questions.explanation: Modals should have role="dialog" and aria-modal="true" to indicate they are modal dialogs that trap focus.

questions.question: How should a tooltip be triggered for accessibility?
questions.explanation: Tooltips must appear on both hover (mouse) and focus (keyboard) to be accessible to all users.

questions.question: What is the minimum touch target size recommended for accessibility?
questions.explanation: WCAG recommends a minimum touch target of 44×44px to accommodate users with motor disabilities.

questions.question: Which ARIA attribute links a tooltip to its trigger element?
questions.explanation: aria-describedby on the trigger element points to the tooltip's ID, providing the tooltip content to screen readers.

questions.question: What should happen to focus when a modal is closed?
options: Focus returns to the element that opened the modal
Focus stays on the close button
questions.explanation: Focus must return to the trigger element when a modal closes, maintaining user context and flow.

questions.question: Which CSS property helps indicate a selected tab?
questions.explanation: border-bottom combined with color provides a visible indicator that doesn't rely on color alone.

questions.question: What is the correct ARIA role for a set of tabs?
questions.explanation: The container of tabs should have role="tablist", with individual tabs having role="tab" and panels role="tabpanel".

questions.question: How should disabled buttons be styled accessibly?
options: Use reduced opacity, no pointer-events, and aria-disabled
questions.explanation: Disabled buttons should have reduced opacity, cursor: not-allowed, pointer-events: none, and aria-disabled="true" for screen readers.

questions.question: Which component needs aria-live region for dynamic updates?
questions.explanation: aria-live regions (polite/assertive) announce dynamic content changes to screen readers without requiring focus.

questions.question: What is the escape pattern for carousel navigation?
questions.explanation: Carousels should support Arrow keys for navigation and have a visible pause/play button for auto-advancing content.

questions.question: Which element is most appropriate for an accordion trigger?
questions.explanation: <button> is the correct semantic element for an accordion trigger as it's natively interactive and keyboard accessible.

questions.question: What CSS technique allows card components to highlight when focused?
questions.explanation: :focus-within on the card container applies styles when any child element inside receives focus.

questions.question: How should you structure form error messages for accessibility?
options: Show all errors at top only
Use aria-describedby on input pointing to error message
questions.explanation: aria-describedby links the input to its error message, ensuring screen readers announce the error when focusing the field.

questions.question: What is the role of a skip link in navigation components?
options: Allow keyboard users to bypass repetitive navigation
questions.explanation: Skip links allow keyboard users to jump directly to main content, bypassing repeated navigation blocks.

questions.question: Which CSS feature helps with loading component states?
questions.explanation: @keyframes with gradient animations create skeleton loading states that indicate content is loading.

questions.question: What should a data table have for accessibility?
options: <caption>, scope on headers, and proper <th> elements
questions.explanation: Accessible tables need <caption>, <th> with scope attributes, and proper table structure for screen reader navigation.

questions.question: Which attribute marks a region as a live region for screen reader updates?
questions.explanation: aria-live="polite" or "assertive" tells screen readers to announce changes to the region's content.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach for accessible custom components?
options: Start with semantic HTML, add ARIA for enhanced semantics, use CSS for presentation
questions.explanation: The golden rule: use native semantic HTML first, add ARIA only when HTML semantics are insufficient, and use CSS purely for presentation.
